methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Inversion Space is a Series A-stage company in Housing & Construction founded by Justin Fiaschetti and Austin Briggs. The company has raised $44M to date. The valuation universe for Housing & Construction is limited, consisting of 4 identifiable private peers (ICON, Cuby Technologies, Cobod, Material Evolution) with no public comparables available.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and anchors the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised of $44M as a proxy for Series A value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $88M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable; while Housing & Construction has limited private peer coverage, none of the peers identified meet minimum criteria for narrow-filter cohort sizing (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier), requiring at least three datapoints for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $75M to $101M, with a median point of $88M. This range reflects the primary reliance on stage-progression methodology given the sparse comparability set. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x step multiplier for this specific sector—Housing & Construction peer dynamics may differ materially from broader venture patterns—and the absence of market-tested pricing signals from either public or recently-funded private peers at comparable valuation tiers. Additional pricing data from sector peers would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
